The Same Methodius to Those Who are Ashamed of the Cross of Christ.

title: The Same Methodius to Those Who are Ashamed of the Cross of Christ.

source.title: ANF06. Fathers of the Third Century: Gregory Thaumaturgus, Dionysius the Great, Julius Africanus, Anatolius, and Minor Writers, Methodius, Arnobius

verses.book: Three Fragments from the Homily on the Cross and Passion of Christ.
verses.text: Some think that God also, whom they measure with the measure of their own feelings, judges the same thing that wicked and foolish men judge to be subjects of praise and blame, and that He uses the opinions of men as His rule and measure, not taking into account the fact that, by reason of the ignorance that is in them, every creature falls short of the beauty of God.

verses.book: Three Fragments from the Homily on the Cross and Passion of Christ.
verses.text: For He draws all things to life by His Word, from their universal substance and nature.

verses.book: Three Fragments from the Homily on the Cross and Passion of Christ.
verses.text: For whether He would have good, He Himself is the Very Good, and remains in Himself; or, whether the beautiful is pleasing to Him, since He Himself is the Only Beautiful, He beholds Himself, holding in no estimation the things which move the admiration of men.

verses.book: Three Fragments from the Homily on the Cross and Passion of Christ.
verses.text: That, verily, is to be accounted as in reality the most beautiful and praiseworthy, which God Himself esteems to be beautiful, even though it be contemned and despised by all else—not that which men fancy to be beautiful.

verses.book: Three Fragments from the Homily on the Cross and Passion of Christ.
verses.text: Whence it is, that although by this figure He hath willed to deliver the soul from corrupt affections, to the signal putting to shame of the demons, we ought to receive it, and not to speak evil of it, as being that which was given us to deliver us, and set us free from the chains which for our disobedience we incurred.

verses.book: Three Fragments from the Homily on the Cross and Passion of Christ.
verses.text: For the Word suffered, being in the flesh affixed to the cross, that He might bring man, who had been deceived by error, to His supreme and godlike majesty, restoring him to that divine life from which he had become alienated.

verses.book: Three Fragments from the Homily on the Cross and Passion of Christ.
verses.text: By this figure, in truth, the passions are blunted; the passion of the passions having taken place by the Passion, and the death of death by the death of Christ, He not having been subdued by death, nor overcome by the pains of the Passion.

verses.book: Three Fragments from the Homily on the Cross and Passion of Christ.
verses.text: For neither did the Passion cast Him down from His equanimity, nor did death hurt Him, but He was in the passible remaining impassible, and in the mortal remaining immortal, comprehending all that the air, and this middle state, and the heaven above contained, and attempering the mortal to the immortal divinity.

verses.book: Three Fragments from the Homily on the Cross and Passion of Christ.
verses.text: Death was vanquished entirely; the flesh being crucified to draw forth its immortality.
